meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Founders
content.seo_description: Stop guessing, start earning. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to validate SaaS pricing models and build profitable products even when you lack ideas.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'perfect' ideas and start building pricing engines that reveal market demand. By architecting a tiered pricing model first, you force yourself to define value propositions. Use Antigravity to scaffold the infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate on revenue-generating logic, turning abstract concepts into high-converting, validated SaaS assets.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the cursor, waiting for a lightning-bolt idea, while the market moves on. The reality is that the most successful founders don't start with an 'idea'; they start with a revenue model. If you don't know how to charge for it, you don't have a business—you have a hobby.

We are going to flip the script. By centering your development around SaaS pricing architecture, you force your product to prove its worth before you write a single line of feature bloat. This is how you bypass the 'no project ideas' trap: by building the machine that captures value first.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Before coding, define three tiers: Entry, Growth, and Enterprise. This forces you to identify the core pain points for different user segments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-tier SaaS pricing model including feature sets, usage limits, and annual vs monthly discount logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a JSON pricing config file based on the schema provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Engine
steps.description: Set up the backend logic to handle recurring billing states, ensuring that your MVP is ready for Stripe integration from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust backend service structure in Node.js to manage Stripe webhooks, user subscription status, and tier-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler and verify the subscription access logic.'

steps.title: Build the Conversion-Focused Landing Page
steps.description: Your landing page must highlight the pricing tiers prominently to test interest before the core app is even complete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting React pricing component with dynamic toggle for monthly/annual pricing and a clear CTA for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the React pricing component and integrate it into the main dashboard view.'

steps.title: Implement Usage Metering
steps.description: To justify your pricing, you need to track user actions. This turns your 'idea' into a service that scales with usage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a middleware for tracking API usage and feature triggers, mapped against the user's current subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject usage tracking middleware into the API routes and link to subscription limits.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your pricing page live to collect emails or pre-orders, effectively validating your project idea through potential revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script for Vercel that includes environment variable configuration for Stripe test keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the final deployment and verify all pricing logic in a staging environment.'

pro_tips: Always start with an annual-first pricing discount to improve your upfront cash flow and customer retention.
Use 'usage-based' pricing as a secondary layer to capture more value as your users scale their own businesses.
Include a 'Contact Sales' button on your highest tier, even if you are a solo founder; it signals enterprise readiness.
Never offer a 'Free' tier unless it is a product-led growth strategy; offer a limited-time 'Trial' instead to maintain perceived value.
Use Claude Code to perform automated 'pricing stress tests' by simulating high-volume subscription changes in your database.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing page is live, share it in niche communities or on X to gather feedback. If no one clicks the 'Subscribe' button, change your target audience, not your code.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering revenue. Your next big thing isn't an idea—it's a validated model. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
